title: Sphenodesme involucrata
taxonomy: Sphenodesme involucrata is a plant species that belongs to the family Rubiaceae. It is a flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. The classification of Sphenodesme involucrata includes its kingdom as Plantae and its order as Gentianales. Sphenodesme involucrata's classification is further divided into its family, genus, and species levels. This plant is a member of the Rubiaceae family, which includes over 13,000 species of flowering plants.

benefits: Sphenodesme involucrata, also known as the 'Kawakawa' tree, has been used in traditional Maori medicine for centuries. intro: Sphenodesme involucrata, also known as Indian snakeroot or Himalayan snakeroot, is a plant species native to the Himalayan region, belonging to the family Lamiaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and snake bites, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic effects.
